role: A client-side SVG/HTML organism generator library that produces token-coloured visual components for the surface interface.
capabilities: source panel visual with real/synthetic asset
substance_why: The file contains ~30 fully implemented generator functions with detailed geometry, deterministic seeded layout, CSS-variable theming, label-fitting, and rich inline SVG/HTML construction.
interactions_missing: no event listeners wired for graph pan/zoom/tap/filter (view-state lives elsewhere)
nav rail buttons expose data-nav but have no click handlers attached here
no keyboard navigation or focus management
no reduced-motion runtime guard beyond CSS class comments
no responsive resize or virtualization for large node/row sets
no error fallback for missing icon SVG injection or malformed data
performance_risks: mesh uses O(n²) nearest-neighbor search per node
graph recomputes full layout and emits large SVG string on every call
unique gradient IDs generated per call (designLibraryObject, fileStack, etc.) can accumulate in DOM
string-concatenation rendering for many nodes/items is memory and GC heavy
boardGroups/constellation render all items unconditionally without virtualization
reuse_why: The organism vocabulary and token-driven rendering concepts are core, but the monolithic string-concatenation factory form should be rebuilt into maintainable components/models.
decomposition_hints: split icon primitives (icon, iconStrip) from data-viz
split network/graph organisms (mesh, hubNetwork, graph, constellation) and their layout engine
split content panels (consequencesBox, cascade, detailStrip, phaseStrip, testimonial, checkMatrix)
split artifact family (designLibraryObject, fileStack, planThumbnail, projectTileGrid, versionStack, sourcePanelVisual, evidenceBand, interfaceFragment, artifactCluster)
extract shared utilities (mulberry32, escape, trim, label-fit, token resolvers) into a separate module
